div1.entryFree: Chain, vb., to bind with a chain; properly and figuratively: Lucr. 900. Tp. IV, 31. Err. IV, 1, 26. H6A II, 3, 39. Rom. IV, 1, 80. Ant. IV, 8, 14 (c. mine armed neck == embrace me). to c. up and restrain the poor, Cor. I, 1, 87 (to fetter them completely). -- Followed by to: affection --s thy tender days to the sweet glances of thy honoured love, Gentl. I, 1, 3. --ed to the ragged staff, H6B V, 1, 203. c. my soul to thine, H6C II, 3, 34.
